title: Saudi-Arabian Lamb Bakrekh
description: A fermented, flaky dish featuring orange blossom water, showcasing the rich flavors of Saudi-Arabian cuisine.
instructions: In a small bowl, mix yeast, salt, black pepper, and 1 cup of lukewarm water to create a yeast starter.
In a large mixing bowl, combine lamb, yeast starter, orange blossom water, parsley, olive oil, onion, ginger, and spices. Mix well.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the mixture ferment at room temperature (22°C/72°F) for 24 hours.
Preheat the sous-vide water bath to 130°F (54°C).
Transfer the lamb mixture to a sous-vide bag and seal it.
Cook the lamb in the sous-vide water bath for 24 hours.
Remove the lamb from the sous-vide bag and let it rest at room temperature for 30 minutes.
Using a very sharp knife, cut the lamb into thin slices.
Heat a non-stick pan over medium heat and add 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
Sear the lamb slices for 2-3 minutes on each side, until golden brown.
Serve the Bakrekh warm, garnished with additional parsley if desired.
